step_by_step_thinking:
I agree that "Drug Induced" (Option C) appears to be the most likely trigger based on the extensive skin and mucosal involvement described, as well as the presence of typical signs such as lip erosions and hemorrhagic crusts. These findings strongly suggest a severe cutaneous adverse reaction consistent with Stevens-Johnson Syndrome or Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis. Given these characteristics, it would indeed make sense to prioritize investigating any recent medications used by the patient. My agreement stands firm because no alternative explanation fits all aspects of the presented case so comprehensively without requiring assumptions beyond what's clinically evident here. **Answer Choice**: C - Drug Induced. 🌟💊🔍👍
